SETTINGS
Session-level parameters — R&D constraints · Module lead times
Module Development Constraints
Floor applied to every module regardless of type or Modify/New path.
Per-type lead time — New path (quarters) — leave blank to use base config defaults
Modify path always gets 50% reduction on top of these values. Global minimum floor still applies.
Resource caps per team per quarter — leave blank for unlimited
If exceeded, module progress slows proportionally or by priority.
Derived from R&D spend (60% FTE share). Both constraints apply independently.
Saved ✓
Industrial Operations Lead Times
Lead time (rounds) for plant investment decisions. Leave blank to use defaults.
Time from decision to plant becoming operational.
Time from decision to expanded capacity coming online.
Q2 Events
⚠ Risk
Steel tariffs +12% — EU–US trade dispute.
→ +€180/vehicle COGS
✦ Opportunity
EV subsidy extended — €3,500 consumer credit through 2026.
→ EV demand +18% if ≤€38k
⚠ Operational
Semiconductor shortage — 8–12 wk lead time increase.
→ Min 30-day buffer advised
◎ Macro
Consumer confidence +4pts. SUV & premium strongest demand.
→ +5% demand SUV/Premium
✦ Regulation
Fleet electrification mandate in 3 EU markets from Q3 2025.
→ Commercial EV demand surge

Instructor Notes
Q2 Focus: EV strategy under cost pressure.

Explore how brand architecture choices (Alpha / Apex / Arrow) can allow differentiated pricing strategies across segments without cannibalisation.

Debrief: How does the 5-year product plan constrain quarterly decisions?
